bird.name: Mangrove kingfisher
bird.summary: The mangrove kingfisher (Halcyon senegaloides) is a kingfisher in the genus Halcyon. It is similar in appearance to the woodland kingfisher.

relations.subject: Mangrove kingfisher
relations.evidence: When not breeding, it lives in estuaries and mangrove, and also wooded shores, thornveld, forest, cultivated land, parks and gardens
relations.object_summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

relations.subject: Mangrove kingfisher
relations.evidence: When not breeding, it lives in estuaries and mangrove, and also wooded shores, thornveld, forest, cultivated land, parks and gardens
relations.object_summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

relations.subject: Mangrove kingfisher
relations.evidence: To breed, it leaves estuaries and mangrove, moving to woodland and wooded rivers; these are generally farther inland, an exception being that they breed in the Zanzibar Archipelago
relations.object_summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

relations.subject: Mangrove kingfisher
relations.evidence: To breed, it leaves estuaries and mangrove, moving to woodland and wooded rivers; these are generally farther inland, an exception being that they breed in the Zanzibar Archipelago
relations.object_summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

relations.subject: Mangrove kingfisher
relations.evidence: The population appears to be declining because of habitat loss, but the decline is not rapid, and the species has a large range, so the IUCN has assessed it as a least-concern species. Juveniles sometimes collide with human structures while migrating
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
